channel.title: MCP on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in MCP on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: MCP Server Knowledge Source in Microsoft Foundry IQ
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry IQ now supports external MCP servers as federated knowledge sources in public preview. This lets teams connect sources from systems like Salesforce, Atlassian, and Confluence without indexing all data themselves.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: MCP toolkit for Azure DocumentDB
item.description:
Azure DocumentDB adds support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP) in public preview. The MCP toolkit lets AI agents and LLM-powered apps connect directly to a DocumentDB database for natural-language querying and document retrieval.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Center now provides a data plane MCP server for enterprise-wide discovery of APIs and AI assets.
item.description:
Azure API Center now includes a data plane MCP server in general availability for enterprise-wide discovery of APIs and AI assets. The update is aimed at helping developers and agents find and connect to enterprise MCP servers, tools, APIs, and AI assets more easily.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Management now supports content safety controls for MCP and A2A APIs.
item.description:
Azure API Management is now generally available with content safety controls for MCP and A2A APIs. It is intended to help organizations apply consistent safety and compliance policies across agent interactions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions now supports hosting MCP Apps
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports hosting MCP apps. The update notes that MCP servers can use richer interactions beyond plain text, including visual data exploration, complex configuration, real-time status monitoring, and multi-step workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Logic Apps MCP Server.
item.description:
Azure Logic Apps MCP Server is now generally available, aimed at making existing workflows callable by AI agents and MCP-based tooling without building custom API layers. The update focuses on reducing integration plumbing and operational complexity.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now generally available includes Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot integration. It supports guided generation of REST, GraphQL, and Model Context Protocol back ends in the development workflow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Announcing Agent Governance Toolkit MCP Extensions for .NET
item.description:
Microsoft announced a public preview .NET package for MCP servers that adds governance features through a single builder extension. The package includes policy enforcement, startup tool scanning, fallback governance, and response sanitization.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Cosmos DB Shell—your new command line for data workflows
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB Shell is in public preview. It adds a command-line interface for querying, exploring, and managing Cosmos DB data, with agentic capabilities powered by Model Context Protocol (MCP).

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Secret scanning with GitHub MCP Server is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning in the GitHub MCP server is now generally available. It can be used with MCP-compatible AI coding agents or IDEs, including GitHub Copilot CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Governing MCP tool calls in .NET with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description:
The post describes how to govern MCP tool calls in .NET using the Agent Governance Toolkit. It focuses on policy-based access control, security scanning, and response sanitization to reduce risk in AI agent systems.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions now supports MCP resource triggers
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports MCP resource triggers for MCP servers hosted on Functions. This lets developers expose resources directly, in addition to tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: SQL MCP Server
item.description:
Azure announced a public preview of SQL MCP Server, an MCP connector for production databases. It is part of Data API Builder and is intended to provide a simple, predictable, and secure way to connect AI agents to data workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates
